channel.title: OT/ICS Threat Intel - Food & Agriculture
channel.description: Curated OT/ICS advisories, incidents and threat intelligence. Editorial by Mike Holcomb in partnership with UtilSec.

item.title: [Critical] CISA flags the Gardyn IoT Hub at CVSS 10.0 — an exposed master key over fleets of precision-ag grow devices
item.description: CISA's most severe fresh advisory for the sector lands on the farm's edge: on 2 July it flagged the Gardyn IoT Hub with a maximum CVSS 10.0 score. The flaws center on an exposed, privileged “iothubowner” key that lets an unauthenticated attacker enumerate every Gardyn device in a fleet, run commands on connected units, and pivot onto the owner's home or facility network; device logs were also left publicly listable in cloud storage. Gardyn's automated indoor-growing systems sit squarely in precision agriculture and controlled-environment farming, and the pattern — cheap, cloud-managed grow-IoT with broad reach — is exactly what is proliferating across the sector. Gardyn says it has fixed the cloud infrastructure and is pushing firmware updates; operators should confirm their devices actually pulled them. - CISA ICS Advisories (ICSA-26-183-03) - Advisory 2 Jul 2026 — CVSS 10.0

item.title: [High] FortiBleed: ~430,000 Fortinet firewalls swept for 110M+ credentials — the way ransomware reaches a processing plant
item.description: CISA is urging Fortinet operators to act after researchers assessed the FortiBleed campaign swept roughly 430,000 internet-facing FortiGate firewalls and SSL-VPN gateways for more than 110 million credentials, now directly linked to active INC Ransom and Lynx ransomware. For food and beverage processors the exposure is the remote-access layer: these appliances frequently front vendor and operator access into plant, packaging and cold-chain networks, and because credentials leaked rather than a bug being exploited, there is no patch to apply. Valid stolen credentials are precisely how ransomware crews — the sector's dominant threat — get in, so treat any exposed device as compromised and rotate now. - CISA (hardening alert) - CISA alert 18 Jun 2026 — active exploitation

item.title: [High] Food and Ag-ISAC: hundreds of ransomware attacks on the sector, led by Qilin and Akira
item.description: The Food and Ag-ISAC's ransomware analysis recorded on the order of 265 attacks against food and agriculture in a single year, with Qilin and Akira responsible for the most intrusions and a handful of groups accounting for nearly half of all sector attacks. Its 2026 outlook expects fragmentation into smaller, specialized crews plus DDoS layering, hypervisor attacks and AI-enabled social engineering. For processors and distributors the practical read is that ransomware — not exotic OT malware — remains the dominant risk, and that segmentation, identity hardening and recoverable backups are what keep a compromise from becoming a production or cold-chain stoppage. - Food and Ag-ISAC / Industrial Cyber - Food and Ag-ISAC 2026 ransomware report

item.title: [Medium] Gardyn Home Kit advisory updated (Update B) alongside the IoT Hub disclosure
item.description: In the same 2 July batch CISA issued an update (Update B) to its Gardyn Home Kit advisory, part of the broader disclosure covering the vendor's consumer and small-scale growing hardware. Operators running Gardyn devices should read the Home Kit advisory together with the IoT Hub advisory, confirm firmware is current, and treat the whole product family as one exposure to remediate rather than isolated devices. - CISA ICS Advisories - Advisory 2 Jul 2026 (Update B)

item.title: [High] CISA flags Frangoteam FUXA SCADA/HMI — lightweight HMI shows up on plant floors too
item.description: CISA's 30 June advisory for Frangoteam FUXA, an open-source SCADA/HMI platform, is tagged for water and energy but matters to food and beverage as well: free, web-based HMI software is attractive to smaller processors and can end up standing in for supervisory control on packaging and process lines. Review the advisory for affected versions, apply the fixes, and make sure any FUXA instance is off the public internet and behind authenticated, segmented access. - CISA ICS Advisories - Advisory 30 Jun 2026

item.title: [Medium] Delta Electronics DVP12SE PLC advisory — common in packaging and batching skids
item.description: CISA's 30 June batch also covered the Delta Electronics DVP12SE programmable logic controller, a compact PLC used across packaging, batching and process skids in food and beverage plants. Confirm whether any DVP12SE units are in your lines, apply the vendor's fixes, and ensure the controllers are segmented away from business and internet-facing networks. - CISA ICS Advisories - Advisory 30 Jun 2026

item.title: [Policy] Australia's enhanced CIRMP rules signal where food-and-grocery cyber obligations are heading
item.description: Australia's Cyber and Infrastructure Security Centre unveiled enhanced Critical Infrastructure Risk Management Program rules on 18 June, expanding obligations to address AI, legacy systems, supply-chain and insider risks across critical sectors that include food and grocery under the SOCI regime. Even for operators outside Australia, the direction of travel is instructive: regulators increasingly expect documented, board-level risk management across the whole food supply chain. Track how these obligations, alongside U.S. FDA/USDA and farm-bill attention, may shape requirements for your operations. - CISC / Industrial Cyber - Announced 18 Jun 2026

item.title: [Policy] NIST SP 1339 OT Backup Quick Start Guide is timely for cold chain and processing
item.description: NIST released an OT Backup Quick Start Guide (SP 1339) on 19 June to help industrial operators build recoverable backups and speed incident recovery. For food and beverage, where a ransomware hit on refrigeration, batching or packaging control can spoil product quickly, tested and isolated backups of process and cold-chain configurations are a food-safety and continuity control. Use it to benchmark your OT backup coverage and recovery timelines. - NIST / Industrial Cyber - Guide released 19 Jun 2026
